tasks.description: Validate and harden the existing graph data pipeline so layout remains deterministic on repeated runs with real and synthetic histories.
steps: Same input history yields stable lane assignment across repeated runs
Real merge-heavy history renders without broken topology

tasks.description: Harden Canvas rendering and viewport behavior for large histories, focusing on visible-range work, redraw stability, and high-density graph readability.
steps: Rapid scroll shows no blank flashes or missing edges
FPS and frame-time behavior stay practical on large histories

tasks.description: Move the graph spike to /spike-b, preserve interaction behavior, and make the route the canonical validation surface for real-repo and synthetic benchmarks.
steps: Root route no longer serves as the primary graph spike surface
Selection, hover, context menu, keyboard navigation, and commit detail behavior still work on /spike-b
Real 10k+ repo benchmark can be performed from the dedicated spike surface

tasks.description: Use the dedicated spike surface to capture explicit evidence that the Canvas graph baseline is production-worthy for the target scale.
steps: Sustained scroll remains practically smooth with observable FPS data
Layout time, lane count, visible rows, and rendering behavior are recorded during manual validation

nonGoals: Replacing Canvas 2D with another rendering technology
